req.title: Card freeze / unfreeze (negative variant)
req.statement: A customer shall freeze or unfreeze their card instantly; frozen cards must reject all authorization attempts. Negative paths (insufficient funds, unauthorised, exceeded limits) must be tested.

doc.reasoning: The requirement covers card freeze/unfreeze with instant state changes, authorization rejection on frozen cards, and error paths. I designed tests across: (1) EP positive — valid card freeze/unfreeze happy path; (2) NEG — frozen card rejects authorization; (3) NEG — 404 on non-existent card; (4) STC — full freeze→authorize→unfreeze→authorize cycle; (5) BVA — card ID at boundary (empty string, extreme length). Each test is self-contained using fixtures for auth tokens and card setup, and relies on idempotent teardown.

tests.name: test_freeze_valid_card_returns_200_and_frozen_state
preconditions: Authenticated customer with valid bearer token
Card belongs to the caller and is currently ACTIVE
Card ID is a valid UUID
actions: PATCH /cards/{id}/freeze with body {"frozen": true} and valid auth header
Assert HTTP 200 and response body contains status=FROZEN
Teardown: unfreeze card to restore state
expected: Response JSON field 'card_id' matches the requested card ID
trace: R-FIN-060: card freeze instantly updates card state
R-FIN-060: expected outcome — card state updated
tests.executable:
import pytest

def test_freeze_valid_card_returns_200_and_frozen_state(
    api_client,       # <fixture>: authenticated httpx/requests session
    active_card,      # <fixture>: yields {"card_id": str, ...} for caller-owned ACTIVE card
):
    card_id = active_card["card_id"]

    resp = api_client.patch(
        f"/cards/{card_id}/freeze",
        json={"frozen": True},
    )

    assert resp.status_code == 200
    body = resp.json()
    assert body["status"] == "FROZEN"
    assert body["card_id"] == card_id

    # Idempotent teardown — restore to ACTIVE
    teardown = api_client.patch(
        f"/cards/{card_id}/freeze",
        json={"frozen": False},
    )
    assert teardown.status_code == 200

tests.name: test_authorization_rejected_on_frozen_card
preconditions: Authenticated customer with valid bearer token
Card belongs to the caller and is currently FROZEN
Sufficient balance exists on the card account
actions: PATCH /cards/{id}/freeze with body {"frozen": true} to ensure frozen state
POST /cards/{id}/authorize with a valid transaction payload
Assert HTTP 403 and authorization_result=DECLINED with reason CARD_FROZEN
trace: R-FIN-060: frozen cards must reject all authorization attempts
R-FIN-060: negative path — authorization on frozen card
tests.executable:
import pytest

def test_authorization_rejected_on_frozen_card(
    api_client,
    active_card,
):
    card_id = active_card["card_id"]

    freeze_resp = api_client.patch(
        f"/cards/{card_id}/freeze",
        json={"frozen": True},
    )
    assert freeze_resp.status_code == 200

    auth_resp = api_client.post(
        f"/cards/{card_id}/authorize",
        json={"amount": 10.00, "currency": "USD", "merchant": "TestMerchant"},
    )

    assert auth_resp.status_code == 403
    body = auth_resp.json()
    assert body["authorization_result"] == "DECLINED"
    assert body["decline_reason"] == "CARD_FROZEN"

    # Teardown
    api_client.patch(f"/cards/{card_id}/freeze", json={"frozen": False})

tests.name: test_freeze_nonexistent_card_returns_404
preconditions: Authenticated customer with valid bearer token
Card ID used does not exist in the system
actions: Assert HTTP 404 and error code CARD_NOT_FOUND
trace: R-FIN-060: error pathway — 404 when card does not exist
tests.executable:
import pytest

NONEXISTENT_CARD_ID = "00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000000"

def test_freeze_nonexistent_card_returns_404(api_client):
    resp = api_client.patch(
        f"/cards/{NONEXISTENT_CARD_ID}/freeze",
        json={"frozen": True},
    )

    assert resp.status_code == 404
    body = resp.json()
    assert body["error_code"] == "CARD_NOT_FOUND"

tests.name: test_freeze_unfreeze_state_transition_cycle
preconditions: Authenticated customer with valid bearer token
Card belongs to the caller and is currently ACTIVE
expected: Card transitions ACTIVE → FROZEN on first call
Card transitions FROZEN → ACTIVE on second call
Authorization succeeds with status APPROVED after unfreeze
trace: R-FIN-060: customer toggles freeze — state transitions covered
R-FIN-060: authorization network notified on state change
tests.executable:
import pytest

def test_freeze_unfreeze_state_transition_cycle(
    api_client,
    active_card,
):
    card_id = active_card["card_id"]

    # ACTIVE → FROZEN
    r1 = api_client.patch(f"/cards/{card_id}/freeze", json={"frozen": True})
    assert r1.status_code == 200
    assert r1.json()["status"] == "FROZEN"

    # FROZEN → ACTIVE
    r2 = api_client.patch(f"/cards/{card_id}/freeze", json={"frozen": False})
    assert r2.status_code == 200
    assert r2.json()["status"] == "ACTIVE"

    # Authorization must succeed now
    r3 = api_client.post(
        f"/cards/{card_id}/authorize",
        json={"amount": 1.00, "currency": "USD", "merchant": "TestMerchant"},
    )
    assert r3.status_code == 200
    assert r3.json()["authorization_result"] == "APPROVED"

tests.name: test_freeze_card_id_at_length_boundary
preconditions: Authenticated customer with valid bearer token
API enforces UUID format (36 chars) for card IDs
actions: PATCH /cards/{id}/freeze with card_id of 35 characters (below UUID boundary) — expect 400 or 404
PATCH /cards/{id}/freeze with card_id of 36 characters (valid UUID boundary) — expect 404 (not found) or 200
PATCH /cards/{id}/freeze with card_id of 37 characters (above UUID boundary) — expect 400 or 422
expected: 35-char ID: HTTP 400 or 404 (invalid format or not found)
36-char valid UUID: HTTP 404 (well-formed but non-existent) or 200 if card exists
trace: R-FIN-060: card ID boundary — UUID format boundary values
BVA applied to card_id length at min-1, min, min+1 of valid UUID length
tests.executable:
import pytest

SHORT_ID  = "00000000-0000-0000-0000-00000000000"   # 35 chars
VALID_ID  = "00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000000"  # 36 chars (valid UUID)
LONG_ID   = "00000000-0000-0000-0000-0000000000000" # 37 chars

@pytest.mark.parametrize("card_id,allowed_statuses", [
    (SHORT_ID,  {400, 404, 422}),
    (VALID_ID,  {200, 404}),
    (LONG_ID,   {400, 404, 422}),
])
def test_freeze_card_id_at_length_boundary(
    api_client,
    card_id,
    allowed_statuses,
):
    resp = api_client.patch(
        f"/cards/{card_id}/freeze",
        json={"frozen": True},
    )
    assert resp.status_code in allowed_statuses, (
        f"Unexpected status {resp.status_code} for card_id length {len(card_id)}"
    )

tests.name: test_freeze_unauthorized_caller_rejected
preconditions: Request is made by customer_B (different authenticated user)
actions: PATCH /cards/{id}/freeze using customer_B token on customer_A card
Assert HTTP 403 or 404 (card not visible to caller)
expected: Error body indicates authorization failure or resource not found
trace: R-FIN-060: precondition — card belongs to caller; negative when it does not
R-FIN-060: error pathway — unauthorised access rejected
tests.executable:
import pytest

def test_freeze_unauthorized_caller_rejected(
    api_client_customer_a,   # <fixture>: session authenticated as customer_A
    api_client_customer_b,   # <fixture>: session authenticated as customer_B
    card_owned_by_a,         # <fixture>: {"card_id": str} owned by customer_A
):
    card_id = card_owned_by_a["card_id"]

    # customer_B attempts to freeze customer_A card
    resp = api_client_customer_b.patch(
        f"/cards/{card_id}/freeze",
        json={"frozen": True},
    )

    assert resp.status_code in {403, 404}

    # Verify card state unchanged via owner
    state_resp = api_client_customer_a.get(f"/cards/{card_id}")
    assert state_resp.status_code == 200
    assert state_resp.json()["status"] == "ACTIVE"
